questions.question: In which of the following periods, copper was first used?
questions.explanation: Chalcolithic period saw the use of the metals among which the Copper was first. Chalcolithic period is also known as Eneolithic period.

questions.question: What is the name of the process used in the manufacture of Harappan seals?
questions.explanation: The process used in the manufacture of Harappan seals is cutting. The seals were made from a stone called steatite and decorated with animal motifs and undeciphered script characters. Artisans carefully cut and shaped small tablets averaging 2 x 2 square inches to produce the seal face design in intaglio, such that stamp impressions made readable reliefs. Variances in seal iconography and craftsmanship indicate localized stylistic preferences and production across Indus urban workshops rather than mass produced standardized seals.

questions.question: Which Harappan site has the most impressive drainage system?
questions.explanation: Of all the excavated Harappan cities, Mohenjo-daro has revealed evidence of the most sophisticated and well-engineered drainage network. It consists of brick-lined sewers built under many streets collecting water and waste from houses and workshops that flowed out through main outlets to avoid flooding. The advanced sanitation planning demonstrated at Mohenjo-daro signifies the Harappan’s ingenuity in civic infrastructure and preventive healthcare policies.

questions.question: What is the Upveda of the Yajur Veda?
questions.explanation: The Yajur Veda majorly deals with the procedure for the performance of sacrifices. The Upveda of the Yajur Veda is called the Dhanur Veda .It contains a collection of short magic spells which is used by a certain class of priests at the time of sacrifices.

questions.question: The marriage of a man in his own varna or below his varna is which type of marriage as mentioned in Chhandogya Upanishad?
questions.explanation: In the Chhandogya Upanishad we can find reference to the first three ashrams. It also discusses the types of marriage. The two main types discussed are the Anuloma marriage and the Pratiloma marriage.

questions.question: At which age Mahavira renounced the world in search of truth?
questions.explanation: Mahavira renounced the world at the age of 30 to become an ascetic and search for truth. He practiced severe penance and meditation for 12 years. At the age of 42, he attained spiritual knowledge. Mahavira was born in 599 BC to a royal family in Kundalpur, India. He married a woman of the Kshatriya caste and had a daughter. He left his family and royal household to become a monk. He spent the next 12 and a half years in deep silence and meditation to conquer his desires, feelings, and attachments. Mahavira achieved enlightenment on the night of Diwali. He preached for 30 years.

questions.question: At which of the following places Gautam Buddha attained Nirvana/Enlightenment?
questions.explanation: Gautam Buddha at the age of 35 attained Nirvana or Enlightenment at Gaya in Bihar under a Peepal tree at Uruvela, on the banks of river Niranjana. He came to be known as the Buddha meaning the Enlightened One.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are correct with respect to Ajivikas?1. Karma is a fallacy.2. Nirvana was only reached after living through an immense number of lives.3. Ajivikas practiced austerities.Select the correct option from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: The following statements are correct with respect to Ajivikas:1. They believed that karma is a fallacy.2. They believed that nirvana can only be reached after living through an immense number of lives.3. Ajivikas practiced austerities.

questions.question: Which of the following is also known as Lokayata?
questions.explanation: Charvaka was the main expounder of the materialistic philosophy which came to be known as Lokayata. Lokayata literally means ‘derived from the local people’. This is one of the oldest school of materialism.

questions.question: Which of the following queens of Ashoka tried to get the Bodhi tree cut?
questions.explanation: Tissarakhita, one of the queens of Ashoka tried to get the Bodhi tree cut. Ashoka is believed to have had a love marriage with his wife Mahadevi who was the daughter of a merchant of Vidisha.
